enrichment.summary: Kyle records a late-night vlog after a long day of data work on his cycling website, mentioning a morning bike ride up a steep mountain road, his daily routine, and his recreational cannabis use since legalization in Colorado.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: mixing both gets you too high; prefers buying premium oil even at high cost because you use less and it lasts longer
big tasks become manageable when you work fast and stay focused — pace adapts to project size
finds it unpalatable, especially day-old without fruit

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder V-LOG with Kyle Pounds. I think it's the 28th of April 2014 at 10.30 at night. I was working all day long and all of a sudden I was like I need to get to sleep and remembered I had to do my daily V-LOG and I was like oh my god, such a switch when I suddenly I'm talking after all day just sitting there.

So what I did today was I had a house up there in the mountain in the foothills so I did right up this really steep road. It got so steep, it was like 14%. I was like barely made it. It's hard to do that kind of stuff where you're at that fit but it was really windy and cold today too.
I was just old riding my bike back and windy. But then I deposited my check and then just came here and worked all day, like all day long. I was just sitting there cutting and pasting all day long.
And from when I came back at like 2 or something until just now, 10.30. So like 8 and a half hours. And I stopped for a little bit to have some salad. I'm going to have to eat some more.
I had something else. Maybe I'll go get a tomato soup now. But I need to get to sleep soon.
Because yeah, I worked at 9 today but tomorrow I'm working at 8 for the first time so I got to set my clock for 6 instead of 7.
I set my clock for 2 hours before I get up. And this is just about enough time, you know, because I leave about 40 minutes before I get there depending on how far it is.
And I lost my train of thought because I'm so high. Yeah. I didn't get in my videos because I'm tired.
Yeah. So, yeah, I've been really high lately. I mean, you know, ever since it legalized, I got my, it's kind of, sometimes you get yourself too high.
Like when you mix the edible with the smokeable. I had some of this stuff last week. This like super hardcore oil and, you know, I ate some of it.
No one else would put in that cap. And anyways, when that empties out, I'm going to buy another one of those for edible.
And then you can put three of them in there. But the good stuff's 95 bucks. It's almost $300 to fill that up.
But, you know, I don't know, man. Maybe it's worth it to get the good stuff. You know, I think I want to do it. Get the good stuff.
Take less hits. The thing lasts longer. Then you forget you spent so much money anyway. It's like your, your, your never ending weed stick.
This battery's cool too. It charges in like five minutes and stays charged all day.
Anyways, I was talking about something, but then I lost my train of thought.
Man.
Yeah. So I was, so I was working all day long and, um, I, oh yeah. Well, when I get up in the morning, yeah, pretty much it takes me all day long.
You know, I mean, it's about the right amount of time.
Yeah. I, you know, cook up my oatmeal and eat it. Although I almost threw up today.
Sometimes when I'm trying to get oatmeal, I literally like barf. I can't eat it.
I think my stomach thinks that it's something wrong out of the way.
If it's like only oatmeal and doesn't have pieces of fruit in it.
Um, but I'm going to give it, but it was a day old oatmeal.
So maybe like somehow, somehow my body knew that it was day old oatmeal, you know, and oatmeal mixed with protein powder isn't very tasty.
Ooh, I ran out of protein powder. Maybe I should go to the supermarket to get that.
Cause I've had the same thing every day.
Uh, yeah, sure.
Um, um, yeah, but I'm really excited about what I'm doing to my site.
I'm really taking this really far with the whole like bike racer level thing.
I went into the Colorado state time trial and I put everybody together and then I'm color coding everything.
So I can compare like who all the different categories compare with each other.
Cause that's going to plug into my little chart I'm making.
And, uh, it's just crazy, uh, ambitious, but I mean, the thing is, is when I, cause I, I, I'm discovering that, um, you know, even like stuff that's really big, that takes a long time.
You can actually do it and you can make it manageable because you just start working really fast.
You will, you know, the speed that you work is in proportion to how like big the project is.
Cause I was just like, there are cut, paste, cut, paste, cut, paste, cut, you know, for like an hour.
And, uh, you know, but, um, it's, uh, you know, anyways, with every day, I get really excited to see like what I, uh, I'm using a thing with the color.
I could, uh, with this that I don't haven't used on the timeline yet.
And, um, yeah, I think I want, I'm going to plug that in.
It's when you put the different colors in the actual background of the letters.
For some reason that wasn't working because it was conflicting with the, um, other ones that I was using.
But I, I, I know there's a way that I can have it to work.
Anyways, so that's that.
That wasn't so bad.
Peace out.
